subject: RFP attached — grid-scale battery analytics platform (Nordwind Energy)
body:
Hello,

Please find attached our formal Request for Proposal for a grid-scale battery storage analytics and optimisation platform. Nordwind Energy is expanding our renewable portfolio across Germany and the Nordics, and we need a partner who can help us forecast, optimise and report on our battery fleet.

The attached RFP contains our scope, scale, integration constraints and evaluation criteria. We would appreciate your intent to respond by the date noted in the document.

Kind regards,
Elena Fischer
Head of Digital Grid, Nordwind Energy AG

document.content:
# Request for Proposal — Grid-Scale Battery Analytics & Optimisation Platform

## 1. About Nordwind Energy AG
Nordwind Energy AG is a renewable independent power producer headquartered in Hamburg, Germany, with approximately 2,400 employees and annual revenue of EUR 1.3 billion. We operate 4.2 GW of wind and solar generation across Germany, Denmark and Norway, and are deploying 900 MWh of grid-scale battery storage over the next 24 months.

## 2. Business Drivers
- Battery revenues today depend on manual day-ahead bidding into EPEX and Nord Pool; we believe we are leaving 12-18% of arbitrage value on the table.
- Regulatory reporting to the Bundesnetzagentur currently takes our team three weeks per quarter.
- We have no unified view of state-of-health across battery sites from three different OEMs (Tesla, Fluence, BYD).

## 3. Scope of Required Capabilities
1. Real-time telemetry ingestion from 11 battery sites (~120,000 signals/sec aggregate).
2. Price forecasting and automated bidding optimisation for EPEX day-ahead and intraday plus Nord Pool.
3. State-of-health and degradation analytics across heterogeneous OEM fleets.
4. Regulatory and ESG reporting with full data lineage.
5. Executive dashboard plus API access for our trading desk.

## 4. Integration Constraints
- Must integrate with our existing SAP S/4HANA and an OSIsoft PI historian.
- Data residency must remain within the EU.
- SSO via our Entra ID tenant; least-privilege access required.

## 5. Scale & Performance
- 11 sites today, growing to 18 within 18 months.
- 900 MWh installed, target 1.6 GWh.
- Sub-second dashboard refresh for the trading desk.

## 6. Commercial
- Indicative budget: EUR 2.5M - 3.5M over three years (platform + services).
- Preferred commercial model: annual subscription plus implementation.

## 7. Evaluation Criteria & Timeline
- Weighted: Technical fit 40%, EU data governance 20%, total cost 20%, delivery track record 20%.
- Intent to respond due within 10 business days; full proposals due in 6 weeks; vendor shortlist of three.
- Target go-live: Q2 2027.

## 8. Key Contacts
- Elena Fischer, Head of Digital Grid (commercial owner & decision maker).
- Dr. Markus Lindqvist, Lead Grid Architect (technical evaluation).

subject: Briefing note — customer data platform discovery (Meridian Retail Group)
body:
Hi,

Ahead of our exploratory conversation, I've attached a short internal briefing note describing where we are with our customer data and what we're trying to achieve. We're early in our thinking and building a business case, so this is more strategic context than a formal spec.

Would value your perspective on approach and rough order of magnitude.

Best,
Tobi Okafor
Director of Data & Analytics, Meridian Retail Group

document.content:
# Internal Briefing Note — Unified Customer Data & Personalisation

**Prepared by:** Data & Analytics, Meridian Retail Group
**Classification:** Internal / Commercial-in-confidence

## Context
Meridian Retail Group operates 480 stores and three e-commerce brands across the UK and Ireland, ~18,000 employees, annual revenue GBP 3.1 billion. Customer data is fragmented across our Salesforce CRM, a legacy loyalty system, Adobe Analytics, and store POS. We cannot resolve a single customer across channels.

## Problem Statement
- ~40% of loyalty members cannot be matched to an online profile.
- Marketing runs batch campaigns with 6-week lead times; no real-time triggers.
- Personalisation on the websites is rules-based and stale.
- Data privacy team has flagged inconsistent consent capture across brands (UK GDPR risk).

## Desired Outcomes
1. A resolved single customer profile across loyalty, web, app and store.
2. Real-time event capture to drive triggered journeys.
3. Consent and preference centralisation for UK GDPR compliance.
4. A measurable lift in repeat purchase rate and email engagement.

## Constraints & Notes
- We are an Azure-leaning shop already (Synapse + Power BI in use).
- No firm budget approved; building the case for FY27. Likely mid-six-figures to low-seven-figures if approved.
- Sensitive: payment data is out of scope and must stay in the existing PCI environment.
- Board is interested in 'AI-driven personalisation' but wary of cost and data governance.

## Stakeholders
- Tobi Okafor, Director of Data & Analytics (sponsor of discovery).
- CMO is the ultimate budget owner.

subject: Post-incident report (shared under NDA) — seeking resilience review
body:
Hello,

Following a recent availability incident, our board has asked us to commission an independent resilience and architecture review. I've attached our internal post-incident report (shared under our mutual NDA) so you have the technical context up front.

We'd like to understand how you'd approach hardening our platform and whether you have relevant payments experience.

Regards,
Hannah Reyes
VP Engineering, AuroraPay

document.content:
# Post-Incident Review — Payment Authorisation Outage

**Company:** AuroraPay (fintech, card-present & online payment processing)
**Severity:** SEV-1 · **Customer impact:** 3h 42m partial outage of authorisation API

## Summary
AuroraPay is a payments processor handling ~4,500 transactions/sec at peak for ~600 merchants across Australia and New Zealand, ~350 employees. On the incident date, a regional failover did not complete, causing elevated decline rates and timeouts on the authorisation path.

## Timeline (AEST)
- 14:02 — Primary region (Australia East) database failover triggered by a storage transient.
- 14:05 — Read replica promotion stalled; connection pool exhaustion on the auth service.
- 14:20 — Manual failover to Australia Southeast initiated.
- 17:44 — Full service restored after cache warm-up and reconciliation.

## Root Causes
1. Single-writer database with no automated cross-region promotion runbook.
2. Auth service lacked circuit breakers; retries amplified load.
3. Observability gaps: no SLO-based alerting on authorisation latency.
4. Disaster-recovery runbook last tested 14 months ago.

## Business Context
- We are pursuing PCI-DSS Level 1 re-certification this year.
- Two large merchants have escalated and requested our resilience roadmap.
- We are scaling into Singapore in the next 12 months and need multi-region by design.

## Ask
Independent review of our resilience posture, a target multi-region architecture on Azure, and a prioritised remediation roadmap. Budget is approved (board-mandated) in the range of AUD 400k-600k for the review and initial remediation.

## Contacts
- Hannah Reyes, VP Engineering (decision maker).
- Daniel Cho, Principal SRE.
